agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. The platform must enforce medical licensing compliance, scheduling integrity, access control over sensitive medical records, and auditability of consultation notes with time-based immutability.","bounded_context":"Appointment Scheduling & Medical Records Access","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled time slot linking exactly one patient to exactly one licensed physician for a virtual consultation, with a defined start and end time."},{"term":"Licensed Physician","definition":"A medical professional whose license number has been verified by the platform and who is therefore eligible to accept patient bookings."},{"term":"License Verification","definition":"The process of validating a physician's medical license number before the platform permits them to receive appointment bookings."},{"term":"Double-Booking","definition":"A scheduling conflict where two or more appointments overlap in time for the same physician. This is an invariant violation."},{"term":"Patient","definition":"An end-user of the platform who books appointments, views their own medical records, and may cancel their own appointments subject to time constraints."},{"term":"Staff Administrator","definition":"A platform operator with elevated privileges, including the ability to cancel appointments inside the 2-hour cancellation window."},{"term":"Treating Physician","definition":"The specific physician assigned to a given appointment, and thereby granted access to that patient's medical records for that consultation."},{"term":"Consultation Notes","definition":"Clinical notes created by the treating physician during or after an appointment. Subject to a 48-hour locking rule after appointment end time."},{"term":"Note Locking","definition":"The irreversible transition of consultation notes to a read-only state, occurring 48 hours after the associated appointment ends."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period ending 2 hours before an appointment's start time, during which a patient may self-cancel."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A patient can only book an appointment with a physician whose license has been verified.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing unlicensed practitioners to see patients is a compliance and patient safety violation. The system is in a corrupt state if this is breached.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: For every appointment in state BOOKED or COMPLETED, appointment.physician.licenseVerified == true"},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A physician cannot have two appointments whose time ranges overlap.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-booking violates physical reality — a physician cannot conduct two simultaneous consultations. Data integrity is corrupt if this occurs.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: For any physician P, no two appointments A1 and A2 exist where A1.start < A2.end AND A2.start < A1.end"},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A patient may cancel their own appointment only if the current time is at least 2 hours before the appointment start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 2-hour threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted (e.g., to 1 hour or 4 hours) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: If a patient-initiated cancellation succeeds, then NOW <= appointment.startTime - 2 hours"},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A staff administrator may cancel any appointment regardless of how close it is to the start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an access-control policy defining elevated privileges. The threshold of who can override is a policy decision.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: A cancellation initiated by a staff administrator succeeds irrespective of time remaining before appointment start"},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A physician's license number must be verified before the physician can accept any bookings.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a regulatory compliance gate. Bypassing it means the platform is operating outside legal bounds — a system integrity violation.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: A physician with licenseVerified == false has zero appointments in any non-cancelled state"},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Patient medical records are visible only to the patient themselves and their treating physician.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data privacy and likely HIPAA-level constraint. Unauthorized access to medical records is a security and compliance breach — the system is in a corrupt state.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Any successful read of patient P's medical records was performed by either P or a physician with a non-cancelled appointment linked to P"},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Consultation notes become read-only (locked) 48 hours after the associated appointment ends.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Clinical record immutability after a defined window is a medical-legal requirement ensuring audit integrity. A note modified after locking means the audit trail is corrupt.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: No write operation on consultation notes succeeds when NOW > appointment.endTime + 48 hours"}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A physician whose license has NOT been verified","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with that physician","then":"The system rejects the booking and returns an error indicating the physician is not available for appointments"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A physician whose license HAS been verified and who has no appointments between 10:00 and 11:00","when":"A patient books an appointment with that physician from 10:00 to 11:00","then":"The appointment is created successfully with status BOOKED"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A physician who has an existing appointment from 10:00 to 11:00","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with the same physician from 10:30 to 11:30","then":"The system rejects the booking and returns a scheduling conflict error"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A patient with an appointment starting at 15:00 and the current time is 12:00 (3 hours before)","when":"The patient requests cancellation of the appointment","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A patient with an appointment starting at 15:00 and the current time is 14:30 (30 minutes before)","when":"The patient requests cancellation of the appointment","then":"The system rejects the cancellation and informs the patient to contact a staff administrator"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A staff administrator and an appointment starting in 15 minutes","when":"The staff administrator requests cancellation of the appointment","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A patient P with medical records and a physician D who is NOT the treating physician for P","when":"Physician D attempts to access patient P's medical records","then":"The system denies access"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A patient P with medical records and physician D who IS the treating physician for P","when":"Physician D attempts to access patient P's medical records","then":"Access is granted and records are displayed"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An appointment that ended at 14:00 on Monday and the current time is 15:00 on Wednesday (49 hours later)","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The system rejects the edit and indicates the notes are locked"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An appointment that ended at 14:00 on Monday and the current time is 13:00 on Wednesday (47 hours later)","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The edit is permitted and saved successfully"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A patient P","when":"Patient P requests to view their own medical records","then":"Access is granted and records are displayed"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A physician who has an existing appointment from 10:00 to 11:00","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with the same physician from 09:00 to 10:00 (adjacent, non-overlapping)","then":"The appointment is created successfully — adjacent slots do not constitute a double-booking"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Physician's license is revoked after appointments are already booked","expected_behavior":"Existing future appointments should be flagged for review or cancellation by a staff administrator. The physician must not be able to accept new bookings. Already-completed appointments remain valid historical records."},{"scenario":"Patient attempts to cancel exactly 2 hours before start time (boundary condition)","expected_behavior":"The rule states 'up to 2 hours before' — cancellation at exactly the 2-hour mark should be permitted. The cutoff is strictly less than 2 hours remaining."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes are being actively edited when the 48-hour locking window expires","expected_behavior":"The system should enforce the lock. Any in-flight save attempted after the 48-hour mark must be rejected. The last successfully saved version before locking becomes the permanent record."},{"scenario":"A physician is both the treating physician and the patient (self-treatment)","expected_behavior":"Access rules still apply: as a patient they see their own records, as treating physician they see consultation records. No special exemption needed — the rules compose naturally."},{"scenario":"Staff administrator attempts to cancel an already-cancelled appointment","expected_behavior":"The system should return an idempotent response or error indicating the appointment is already cancelled. No state change occurs."},{"scenario":"Appointment is cancelled — does the treating physician retain access to medical records?","expected_behavior":"Access should be scoped to the appointment relationship. If the appointment is cancelled, the physician is no longer the treating physician for that encounter and access should be revoked unless another active appointment exists."},{"scenario":"Two concurrent booking requests for the same physician's last available slot","expected_behavior":"The system must use optimistic or pessimistic concurrency control to ensure only one booking succeeds. The other must receive a conflict error. Double-booking invariant must hold under concurrency."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes are empty — does the 48-hour lock still apply?","expected_behavior":"Yes. The lock applies to the notes resource regardless of content. After 48 hours, even creating initial notes for that appointment must be rejected."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Failure to verify physician licenses exposes the platform to malpractice liability and regulatory fines. Double-booking wastes physician time and causes patient dissatisfaction leading to churn. Cancellation policies balance patient flexibility against physician revenue protection.","operational":"License verification is a prerequisite gate that affects physician onboarding velocity. The 48-hour note-locking window defines the clinical documentation SLA — physicians must complete notes within that window or lose the ability to do so. Double-booking prevention requires robust concurrency handling in scheduling.","user":"Patients expect reliable scheduling, transparent cancellation rules, and strict privacy of their medical records. Physicians expect a clear documentation window and assurance they will not be double-booked. Staff administrators need override capabilities for edge-case cancellations.","risk":"HIPAA or equivalent privacy violations from improper medical record access could result in regulatory action, lawsuits, and reputational damage. Unlicensed physician bookings create legal liability. Note-locking failures compromise the medical-legal audit trail.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Patients","impact":"Scheduling reliability, cancellation flexibility, strict privacy of medical records, trust in platform compliance"},{"group":"Physicians","impact":"Protected from double-booking, clear 48-hour documentation window, license verification as a trust signal"},{"group":"Staff Administrators","impact":"Granted override authority for late cancellations, responsible for managing edge cases like license revocations"},{"group":"Compliance / Legal Team","impact":"License verification and record access controls are their primary concern; note immutability supports audit defensibility"},{"group":"Platform Operator","impact":"Regulatory exposure from non-compliance; scheduling integrity directly affects platform reliability and reputation"}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is well-defined with clear, non-contradictory rules. All six stated rules are unambiguous and internally consistent. Confidence is not higher because the spec is silent on several important operational details (noted in low-confidence areas) that would matter in production but are not strictly required for the stated rules.","high_confidence_areas":["Physician license verification as a booking prerequisite","Double-booking prevention invariant","Patient vs staff administrator cancellation authority split","Medical record access control scoping","48-hour consultation note locking rule"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the 2-hour cancellation boundary is inclusive or exclusive (assumed inclusive — 'up to 2 hours' means >= 2 hours remaining is allowed)","Whether cancelled appointments revoke treating-physician access to medical records","What happens to future appointments when a physician's license is revoked","Whether 'treating physician' access persists after the appointment is completed or only during an active appointment window","Regulatory framework assumed (HIPAA-like) but not explicitly stated"],"assumptions":["The 2-hour cancellation cutoff is inclusive: cancellation is allowed when time remaining >= 2 hours","Consultation note locking is enforced at the system level, not advisory","Treating physician access to records is scoped per appointment relationship","The platform operates under HIPAA or similar medical privacy regulations","Appointment time ranges are half-open intervals: [start, end) for overlap calculation"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
